name: Cheese-Stuffed Baked Pasta Shells
recipeIngredient: 1 1/4 cups no salt added tomato sauce
1 1/2 ounces mozzarella cheese, part skim milk, shredded
recipeInstructions:
1. Bring a large pot of water to a boil. Add the pasta and cook until al dente, 8 to 10 minutes, or according to package directions. Drain the pasta in a colander, rinse under cold running water, and set aside to drain again.
2. Meanwhile, warm the oil in a medium saucepan over medium-high heat until hot but not smoking. Add the onion and garlic, and cook, stirring, until the onion begins to brown, 2 to 3 minutes. Add the tomato sauce, half of the basil, and pepper, and bring to a boil. Reduce the heat to low and simmer the sauce, uncovered, for 10 minutes.
3. Preheat the oven to 375°F.
4. In a medium bowl, combine the ricotta, Parmesan cheese, egg, and remaining basil. Divide evenly among the shells and stuff each shell with the ricotta mixture.
5. Place the stuffed shells in a shallow 11 by 7 inch baking dish. Pour the tomato sauce over the shells and sprinkle with mozzarella. Bake for 25 minutes, or until hot and bubbly. Sprinkle with chopped parsley if desired.
Serving Size: 7 stuffed shells plus sauce (I only got 4 stuffed shells with sauce).
Each serving provides: 1/2 fat, 2 proteins, 1/2 vegetable (modified to new program), 2 breads.
Nutrition information, Values are approximate per serving: 359 calories, 20 g protein, 13 g fat, 41 g carbohydrate, 68 mg cholesterol, 222 mg sodium. Serve with slices of fresh fennel on a bed of assorted salad greens, drizzled with a simple vinaigrette. For dessert, present a bowl of chilled strawberries sprinkled with Marsala wine and crushed macaroons. For a spicy variation, substitute shredded jalapeno jack cheese for half of the mozzarella and add 1/2 teaspoon ground cumin to the sauce.
